turns.content: Good afternoon, and welcome to the Amazon.com Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered an outstanding quarter with results exceeding our expectations across every segment. In Q1 2024, Revenue grew 21% year over year, significantly ahead of consensus estimates. Our AI services strategy continues to gain traction, with AWS showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in AI services because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from logistics validate our approach. Our pipeline is the strongest it has ever been, and we are raising our full-year guidance accordingly.

Thank you, Andy. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2024 was $85.1 billion, representing a decline of 4% year over year. Our AWS revenue reached $14.4 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin was 39.2%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $25.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $4 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For AWS, we saw 15% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Prime?

Thanks, Mark. Great question. On Prime membership, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in cost optimization has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Prime to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Brian for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Wamsi. The free cash flow trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating income trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Kash. The operating income trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Amazon.com Inc. Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

Our results this quarter reflect a more difficult operating environment than we anticipated. In Q2 2024, Revenue declined 23% year over year to $79.6B, impacted by softening demand in key segments. Our advertising growth strategy continues to gain traction, with AWS showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in advertising growth because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from logistics validate our approach. Given the current visibility, we are revising our guidance range to reflect a more conservative scenario.

Thank you, Andy. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2024 was $59.2 billion, representing growth of 1% year over year. Our AWS revenue reached $7.4 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 41.8%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $18.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $13 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For AWS, we saw 15% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in AWS?

Thanks, Wamsi. Great question. On Prime membership, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position AWS for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect AWS to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Brian for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in AWS?

Sure, Erik. The operating income trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Prime?

Sure, Shannon. The Prime membership trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Keith. Great question. On Prime membership, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Prime for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Prime to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Brian for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Amazon.com Inc. Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

I am extremely pleased with our performance this quarter. The momentum we are seeing is unprecedented. In Q3 2024, We saw 10% growth in AWS, well above our internal targets. Our advertising growth strategy continues to gain traction, with AWS showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in advertising growth because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from logistics validate our approach. Our pipeline is the strongest it has ever been, and we are raising our full-year guidance accordingly.

Thank you, Andy. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $33.4 billion, representing growth of 25% year over year. Our AWS revenue reached $36.1 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 36.3%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $17.4 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $10 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For AWS, we saw 15% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating income trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Brad. Great question. On operating income, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in fulfillment efficiency has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Alexa to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Brian for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating income trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Amit. Great question. On operating income, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in cost optimization has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Alexa to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Brian for the specific numbers.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the AWS revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Erik. Great question. On AWS revenue, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in fulfillment efficiency has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Ring to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Brian for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Amazon.com Inc. Q4 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This was a record-breaking quarter for the company, reflecting the strength of our strategy and execution. In Q4 2024, Revenue grew 9% year over year, significantly ahead of consensus estimates. Our cost optimization strategy continues to gain traction, with Ring showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in cost optimization because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from cloud computing validate our approach. Thank you, Andy. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q4 2024 was $53.2 billion, representing growth of 12% year over year. Our AWS revenue reached $20.5 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 45.5%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $15.3 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $13 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For AWS, we saw 24% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Keith. The operating income trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Ring?

Thanks, Raimo. Great question. On free cash flow, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in advertising growth has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Ring to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Brian for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On Kindle, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Karl. The free cash flow trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $21.1 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $12 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For AWS, we saw 24% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
I wanted to ask about AI services. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Amit. The free cash flow trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Wamsi. The free cash flow trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Toni. The operating income trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Amazon.com Inc. Q2 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered solid results this quarter, broadly in line with our expectations. In Q2 2025, We achieved 27% growth in Alexa, which is in the range we expected given current market conditions. Our cost optimization strategy continues to gain traction, with Alexa showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in cost optimization because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from logistics validate our approach. We are maintaining our full-year guidance and continue to invest in long-term growth opportunities.

Thank you, Andy. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2025 was $111.7 billion, representing growth of 26% year over year. Our AWS revenue reached $21.6 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 38.3%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $21.9 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $4 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For AWS, we saw 5% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
I wanted to ask about advertising growth. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Brad. The operating income trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating income trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Kash. Great question. On operating income, we are pleased with the progress on advertising growth and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Ring to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Brian for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Wamsi. The operating income trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about fulfillment efficiency. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Raimo. Great question. On free cash flow, we are pleased with the progress on fulfillment efficiency and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Prime to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Brian for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.
